title: vaccinium shastense subsp. shastense
intro: Vaccinium shastense subsp. shastense, also known as the Shasta blueberry, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Shasta-Trinity Alps region in California. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Vaccinium shastense subsp. shastense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Traditionally, the leaves and berries of this plant have been used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, which can be made by infusing the dried leaves or berries in hot water.
